reasoning_model_1: Model 1 fails significantly in Content Accuracy. The generated audio is incomplete, abruptly cutting off at '原来是一只s' instead of finishing the required sentence '...小猫，你这个小家伙怎么跑到这里来了？'. This truncation makes the audio unusable. Regarding the paralinguistic feature 'Non-verbal vocalizations' (Scream), the text representation '啊，' with a comma suggests a short, potentially flat vocalization rather than the prolonged, intense scream requested ('啊——！'). Due to the severe content failure and lack of paralinguistic intensity, the model performs poorly.
reasoning_model_2: Model 2 demonstrates excellent Content Accuracy, correctly generating the full script without omissions or errors. Regarding the paralinguistic feature 'Non-verbal vocalizations', the model successfully incorporates the requested 'scream' at the beginning, represented by the prolonged '啊——！'. The audio likely conveys the high intensity of sudden shock followed by the necessary transition to a relieved and affectionate tone for the latter part of the sentence ('原来是一只小猫...'). The paralinguistic execution aligns well with the dramatic nature of the prompt.
model_comparison: Model 2 is the clear winner. Model 1 suffers from a critical failure in content generation, stopping mid-sentence with a hallucinated or cut-off ending ('一只s'). This renders the audio invalid regardless of its tone. In contrast, Model 2 provides the complete content and, based on the transcript conventions, effectively executes the required 'scream' paralinguistic feature (indicated by the elongation '——'), showing superior control over both content and style.
